question_text: Which statement correctly compares the gravitational field strengths on Earth and the Moon?

options.label: A
options.text: Earth's field is stronger because it has greater mass and a larger radius

options.label: B
options.text: The Moon's field is stronger because it is closer to the Sun

options.label: C
options.text: They are equal because both are planets in space

options.label: D
options.text: Earth's field is weaker because objects weigh less there

correct_answer: Earth's field is stronger because it has greater mass and a larger radius
explanation: Gravitational field strength depends on both an object's mass and its radius. Earth has much more mass than the Moon, which creates a stronger gravitational field. Although Earth's larger radius would normally decrease field strength, Earth's significantly greater mass has a dominant effect. This is why Earth's gravitational field strength (~10 N/kg) is about 6 times stronger than the Moon's (~1.6 N/kg), which is why objects weigh more on Earth than on the Moon.
